name: Bullyard Solar Farm

timeline.title: COD estimate updated
timeline.detail: COD changed from 2027-04 to 2027-04-30 (source: AEMO Generation Information)

scheme_contracts.execution_notes: European Energy Australia's second T7 win (with Kayuga NSW). Construction start mid-2026 imminent (6-12 months) — candidate to reach cisa_signed / fid next among T7 non-NSW cohort. Same EEA pattern as operating Mokoan Solar (T1 award ops Jun 2025).

operational_notes: European Energy Australia (Danish parent) is CIS counterparty. SPV 'EE Solar 9 Pty Ltd' is operating entity — same EE Solar # pattern as operating Mokoan Solar (EE Solar 6 Pty Ltd, T1 award, ops Jun 2025). Bullyard is EEA's second T7 win alongside Kayuga (NSW). **Construction start mid-2026 imminent (6-12 months) — candidate to reach cisa_signed / fid next among T7 non-NSW cohort.**

timeline_events.title: CIS Tender 7 underwriting awarded (97 MW)
timeline_events.detail: Solar-only — 97 MW awarded under CIS T7 (DCCEEW announcement). European Energy Australia's second T7 win.

sources.title: New projects to deliver 7.8 GW of clean energy (DCCEEW)
Australia's biggest wind project and 8 battery hybrids among 19 winners (RenewEconomy)
